Best Match & Bank Comparison

Indicative public rates. Confirm with bank before applying.

What is a Mortgage?

A mortgage is a long-term loan used to buy property. You pay a deposit upfront, and the bank finances the balance. You then repay monthly with interest over an agreed period.

  • Deposit: Your upfront contribution (usually 15%-40%).
  • LTV: Loan-to-Value. Higher LTV means lower deposit, but often stricter qualification.
  • Term: How long you repay (e.g. 10 to 25 years).

Mortgage Terms Explained

Interest Rate

The annual cost charged by the bank on the outstanding loan. Even small rate differences can significantly change monthly repayments.

Amortization

How your monthly payment is split between interest and principal over time. Early payments are usually more interest-heavy.

Arrangement Fee

A one-time setup fee charged by the bank, often a percentage of the approved loan amount.

Early Repayment

Some lenders charge a fee if you clear the loan early. Always check this before signing your offer letter.

Daily Update Flow

Rates are pulled from our API and reviewed daily. Use the refresh button above for the latest available data.

Need a mortgage consultant call-back?

Share your details and we will connect you to a mortgage consultant for guidance.

This is a lead request, not a bank approval. Rates and approvals are subject to lender checks.

Fraud Prevention

We use layered verification, AI checks, and manual moderation to keep makaug safer for buyers, renters, brokers, and property listers.

🪪

Identity Verification

OTP + National ID checks before listing submission and publishing review.

🤖

AI Fraud Signals

Image duplication checks, suspicious listing patterns, and document anomaly checks.

👀

Manual Moderation

Every new listing is reviewed before going live. High-risk entries are escalated.

🏛️

Owner Registration Review

Verified owner status is added by admin only after follow-up checks, including Ministry of Lands confirmation where needed.

How our review flow works

  1. 1Submit: Listing details, photos, contact info, and verification data are captured.
  2. 2Automated checks: We run OTP checks, format checks, risk scoring, and duplication detection.
  3. 3Team moderation: High-confidence listings are approved; suspicious ones are held or rejected.
  4. 4Decision + notify: Lister receives status and reason. Approved listings publish to public pages.
  5. 5Registration review: MakaUg admin controls owner and broker verification badges after supporting checks are complete.
We cooperate with lawful authority requests under Ugandan law where fraud, forgery, or impersonation is suspected.

Safety tips for users

  • Never pay before viewing a property physically.
  • Verify ownership/title details before any deposit.
  • Use registered brokers for high-value transactions.
  • Report suspicious listings immediately with screenshots.
WhatsApp Fraud Team
Review Snapshot

What our fraud detection looks at before a listing goes live

The system checks verification, duplicated content, image quality, and suspicious behavior signals before an admin can approve a listing.

Live moderation logic
Green = clear, amber = review, red = blocked until fixed or overridden by admin review.
Identity + OTP check
PASS

Phone/email OTP, ID number format, and document availability are checked together.

Duplicate content scan
REVIEW

We compare title, location, ID reuse, and repeated image patterns before publishing.

Image + location quality
PASS

Minimum photo count, usable uploads, and map pin / location consistency are verified.

Decision + outreach
BLOCK IF FAIL

If checks fail, the lister receives a reason. If approved, MakaUg sends live and sharing links.

Moderation console preview
Example of the checks used before approval
QA style view
OTP verification completed
Green
Phone/email confirmed for the same listing contact details.
Previous lister history checked
Warning
The system found earlier listings and flags them for admin review, not instant rejection.
Critical mismatch / fraud signal
Fail
Blocking failures stop approval until fixed, or until reviewed and explicitly overridden in admin.

What we monitor continuously

Listing quality

Misleading titles, fake pricing, copied descriptions.

Media checks

Stolen images, repeated images, poor or suspicious uploads.

Identity integrity

NIN format mismatch, OTP mismatch, impossible contact patterns.

Behavior risk

Bulk spam, repeated complaint signals, repeat fraud patterns.

Create your free makaug account to:
  • Track listing views, saves, and enquiries in real time.

Property Details

You can change language any time from the selector at the top bar.

Changed the property type? Use “Clear & Start Again” if this is a different listing.

Location setup (step-by-step)

  1. 1. Select region, then district.
  2. 2. Select city/town, then neighbourhood/zone.
  3. 3. Confirm area, street, and optionally type full address.
  4. 4. Confirm the map pin matches your property location.

Pin Property Location

Due to limited formal addresses, place a map pin for best search accuracy. District/city/neighbourhood info recenters the map automatically.

Resolved Map Location
Set a pin to resolve the nearest location label.
Example: "Modern 3-bedroom bungalow in Ntinda, 5 minutes from shopping centre. 2 bathrooms, secure perimeter wall, paved compound, borehole water, and backup generator. Quiet residential street with easy taxi access."

Photos & Media

Upload clear photos. At least 5 photos are required, and you can upload up to 20. Better quality images increase trust and enquiries.

Choose one image as the main photo. This will appear first in search results.

Rejected Listings & Reasons

Sourced Listings

Contract & Terms

  • Field payout is based on listings successfully approved and published.
  • Fraudulent or unverifiable listings are rejected and not payable.
  • Payout runs weekly on Friday based on prior cut-off period.
  • Escalations and compliance events may suspend payout pending review.

About makaug.com

Uganda's free property platform built to make listing, searching, and connecting safer, faster, and more transparent across all 146 districts.

Coverage
146 Districts

From Kampala and Wakiso to emerging regional growth corridors.

Model
100% Free Listing

Owners and brokers can publish without listing fees.

Access
Web + WhatsApp

Designed for smartphone-first usage in Uganda.

Our Mission

makaug.com exists to modernize Uganda's property marketplace by improving listing quality, reducing fraud risk, and making verified property information easier to access for buyers, renters, investors, and brokers.

We combine local market knowledge with practical digital tools: map-based search, district-level filters, and WhatsApp-assisted listing for users who prefer mobile workflows.

Our goal is simple: make it easier to move from enquiry to verified viewing, and from verified viewing to informed decision-making.

What We Provide

  • Property discovery for sale, rent, student housing, commercial units, and land.
  • Direct contact with owners or AREA-verified brokers.
  • Clear listing status indicators (private listing vs broker listing).
  • Structured listing forms designed around Ugandan market realities.
  • Support channels via phone, email, and WhatsApp.

Trust and Compliance Focus

  • Identity verification processes for listers and broker profile validation.
  • Fraud reporting workflow with review and escalation.
  • Privacy and data-handling controls aligned with Ugandan legal requirements.
  • Safety guidance for inspections, payments, and documentation checks.
  • Internal review controls before publishing sensitive listing changes.

Uganda Market Context

Uganda's property market includes multiple tenure and transaction structures. Users should always conduct due diligence, including title verification, contract review, and lawful payment trails. For regulated steps such as stamp duty assessment, registration, and title processing, always confirm current requirements with the relevant government authorities.

makaug.com is a listing and discovery platform. We do not act as legal counsel, escrow provider, or party to sale or tenancy contracts.
